subject: [PATCH v6 17/20] nfsd: add the filehandle to returned attributes in CB_NOTIFY

2026-06-11.patch_summary: This patch adds a new routine to compose a filehandle for CB_NOTIFY operations, which do not have access to the usual svc_export. The new routine uses only the parent filehandle and nfs4_file to fill out the fhandle field in the nfsd4_fattr_args. This allows the filehandle to be returned as an attribute in CB_NOTIFY responses.

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary: Identified a potential issue with the constructed filehandle attribute in CB_NOTIFY operations, where it may not be properly signed or MACed. Recommended either signing the constructed handle or suppressing the attribute when the export requires signed filehandles.

2026-06-12.patch_summary: This patch series adds support for returning the filehandle in CB_NOTIFY operations, which is necessary because nfsd's usual fh_compose routine requires a svc_export and fills out a svc_fh, but there is no such export available in the context of a CB_NOTIFY. A new routine is added to compose a filehandle with only a parent filehandle and nfs4_file, and this is used to fill out the fhandle field in the nfsd4_fattr_args.
